From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2010 SAFF Women's Championship

| Bangladesh 2010 | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Tournament details | |
| Host country | Bangladesh |
| Dates | 12–24 December |
| Teams | 8 (from 1 confederation) |
| Venue | 1 (in 1 host city) |
| Final positions | |
| Champions | [India](/wiki/India_women%27s_national_football_team "India women's national football team") (1st title) |
| Runners-up | [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal_women%27s_national_football_team "Nepal women's national football team") |
| Tournament statistics | |
| Matches played | 15 |
| Goals scored | 94 (6.27 per match) |
| Top scorer(s) | [India](/wiki/India "India") [Sasmita Mallik](/wiki/Sasmita_Mallik "Sasmita Mallik") (14 goals) |
| Best player | [India](/wiki/India "India") [Bala Devi](/wiki/Bala_Devi "Bala Devi") |
| [2012](/wiki/2012_SAFF_Women%27s_Championship "2012 SAFF Women's Championship") → | |

International football competition

In 2010, the first edition of the [SAFF Women's Championship](/wiki/SAFF_Women%27s_Championship "SAFF Women's Championship") was held from 12 to 23 December 2010 in Bangladesh. It was organised by the [South Asian Football Federation](/wiki/South_Asian_Football_Federation "South Asian Football Federation").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

India won the title without conceding a goal.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Though held in the same year in Bangladesh, and also India winning over Nepal, the tournament is not to be confused with the women's football event for the [2010 South Asian Games](/wiki/2010_South_Asian_Games "2010 South Asian Games").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

| * [Bala Devi](/wiki/Bala_Devi "Bala Devi")  18', 31', 32' * [Gayatri](/w/index.php?title=Gayatri_Mallick&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gayatri Mallick (page does not exist)")  26', 39' * [Sasmita](/wiki/Sasmita_Mallik "Sasmita Mallik")  33' * [Amoolya](/wiki/Amoolya_Kamal "Amoolya Kamal")  45' * [Manpreet](/wiki/Manpreet_Kaur_(footballer) "Manpreet Kaur (footballer)")  88' | [Report](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/sports/football/top-stories/india-rout-pakistan-8-0-in-saff-womens-cships-final/articleshow/7133955.cms#:~:text=Bala%20Devi%20fired%20in%20a,the%20inaugural%20SAFF%20Women's%20Championships.) |  |
